title: sambucus racemosa var. microbotrys
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. microbotrys, also known as the dwarf or short-bracted elderberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating colds, flu, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Sambucus racemosa var. microbotrys have been found to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and alleviating symptoms of allergies. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and syrups, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including sinus infections, sore throats, and bronchitis.
